meta_title: For Students: email automation to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to email automation and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Email Automation to Solve 'No Project Ideas'
content.seo_description: Overcome project block with this beginner-friendly guide to email automation. Build your portfolio using Antigravity and Claude Code in five simple steps.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for the 'perfect' idea. Build an automated email scheduler that tracks specific web events or news. This project teaches you API integration, cron jobs, and data handling—essential skills for any developer. By automating a workflow, you prove you can solve real-world problems, instantly boosting your portfolio's value.
content.intro:
The screen stares back at you, blank and indifferent. You have the skills, you have the hunger, but you lack the map. Most students spend months trapped in 'tutorial hell' because they think they need a billion-dollar idea to start. The truth? You don't need a unicorn; you need a system that works.

Email automation is the silent engine of the modern internet. By building a tool that monitors data and dispatches intelligent, automated insights, you aren't just writing code—you are building utility. This project is your gateway to understanding how to bridge the gap between static scripts and living, breathing applications.

steps.title: Define the Data Source
steps.description: Select a target API (like Hacker News or a weather service) to trigger your emails. Focus on simplicity to ensure you finish the MVP quickly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Python script that fetches the top 5 trending headlines from the Hacker News API and parses the JSON response for titles and URLs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --file fetch_data.py --task 'Integrate Hacker News API and print results to console'

steps.title: Configure SMTP Transport
steps.description: Learn how to authenticate with an email provider. Use environment variables to protect your credentials—a mandatory habit for any professional.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a modular Python function using smtplib to send a plain-text email with a subject line and body content, using environment variables for authentication.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --package python-dotenv --task 'Setup secure SMTP credential loading'

steps.title: Template Your Content
steps.description: Transform raw data into a readable format using Jinja2 templates. This separates your logic from your visual output, a key architectural pattern.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Jinja2 HTML template that iterates through a list of article dictionaries and renders them into a clean, professional email layout.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write --file email_template.html --template 'Jinja2 layout for tech newsletter'

steps.title: Implement the Scheduler
steps.description: Move beyond manual execution. Use a scheduler to run your script at specific intervals, simulating a production-grade backend service.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Explain how to use the 'schedule' library in Python to run the main automation script every morning at 8:00 AM.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file main.py --add 'Schedule execution loop using schedule library'

steps.title: Deploy and Monitor
steps.description: Deploy your script to a cloud instance. Learn to check logs to ensure your automation triggers reliably without human intervention.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a Dockerfile and a basic deployment checklist for running a Python script as a background service on a Linux server.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create Dockerfile for production deployment'

pro_tips: Always use a dedicated 'App Password' for Gmail/Outlook rather than your primary login credentials.
Implement basic error handling with try-except blocks to ensure your script alerts you via log if an API call fails.
Store your data in a simple SQLite database if you want to prevent sending the same link twice to the same user.
Keep your email body responsive by using inline CSS; many email clients strip external stylesheets.
Use a logging library instead of print statements to track the history of your automation's performance.
content.next_steps: Once your script is running reliably, add a layer of personalization by using an LLM API to summarize the articles before sending the email.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped with the workflow to turn an idea into a deployed reality. Stop dreaming and start executing. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
